stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML domains like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The presence of Llama and Facebook tags indicates relevance to major industry players in large language models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of transformer-based text generation models.
The associated tags indicate relevance to key ML components like 'transformers' and 'safetensors'.

stories.title: Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Corey Feld
bullet_points: LLM-powered research agents struggle with verifiable source attribution, despite synthesizing information from numerous web sources.
Existing methods are flawed, either trusting potentially biased self-citation or using RAG without validating source accessibility or factual consistency.
The paper introduces a novel source attribution evaluation framework utilizing a reproducible AST parser to address this critical verification gap.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face platform.
The model is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformers and safetensors.
It is associated with the deepseek_v3 family and is relevant to conversational AI applications.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports running tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using local or cloud models.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to deploy gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
stories.author: Justine Calma
bullet_points: AI data centers are the essential physical infrastructure driving the current AI boom.
The rapid expansion of these energy-intensive facilities is causing global conflicts.
Key concerns surrounding data center growth include strain on power grids, environmental impact, and community opposition.

stories.title: Apple reportedly has a deal to use Intel-made chips again
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Apple is reportedly re-engaging with Intel for chip manufacturing.
This marks a potential reversal or expansion from Apple's successful shift to proprietary Apple Silicon.
The agreement is described as 'preliminary,' suggesting ongoing negotiations.

stories.title: Course correction: Google to link more sources in AI Overviews
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is enhancing its AI Overviews feature by improving source citation.
This change aims to make the AI search results more transparent and verifiable.
The update represents a course correction in how Google integrates and attributes information from various sources.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for fine-grained control in video generation, addressing both character motion and camera cinematography.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a novel scene while allowing per-frame control over intrinsic and extrinsic camera parameters.
The model is designed to be compatible with any pretrained image-to-video diffusion model that accepts scene depth conditioning.

stories.title: AI Co-Mathematician: Accelerating Mathematicians with Agentic AI
stories.author: Pushmeet Kohli
bullet_points: Introduction of an 'AI Co-Mathematician,' a workbench designed to aid mathematicians in open-ended research.
The system leverages AI agents to support the entire mathematical workflow, from ideation and literature search to theorem proving.
Key features include an asynchronous, stateful workspace capable of managing uncertainty and tracking failed hypotheses.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for fine-grained control in video generation, addressing both character motion and cinematography.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a new scene while allowing per-frame control over camera parameters (intrinsic and extrinsic).
The model is designed to be plug-and-play, building upon any pre-trained image-to-video diffusion model conditioned on scene depth.

stories.title: Why Global LLM Leaderboards Are Misleading: Small Portfolios for Heterogeneous Supervised ML
stories.author: Swati Gupta
bullet_points: The paper challenges the validity of current global LLM leaderboards, which rely on pairwise human feedback.
Analysis of 89K comparisons across 52 LLMs shows that the best-fit Bradley-Terry ranking is misleading.
The study concludes that the top 50 ranked models are statistically indistinguishable, with pairwise win probabilities barely exceeding 0.53.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM platforms and proprietary API services.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's working directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Amazon is adding a vertical video feed to Prime Video
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Amazon Prime Video is integrating a vertical, short-form video feed called "Clips."
This feature mirrors trends seen on platforms like TikTok, Netflix, and Disney Plus.
Users can access full titles, rent, or purchase content directly from the short-form clips.

stories.title: Here is Yarbo’s promise to fix the robot mower that ran me over
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Robot lawnmowers from Yarbo have been vulnerable to hacking.
The vulnerability allows hackers to access sensitive user data, including GPS coordinates and passwords.
Yarbo has issued a statement promising to fix the security flaws in their devices.

stories.title: Manufacturing qubits that can move
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The article addresses the technical challenge of integrating electronic manufacturing with flexible geometries.
The focus is on the difficulty of manufacturing mobile or movable qubits.
This points to ongoing hurdles in advancing quantum computing hardware.

stories.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A cyberattack has disrupted the Canvas learning platform across various schools and colleges.
The incident was timed to coincide with year-end finals, leading to widespread disruption.
As a result of the attack, educational institutions nationwide have postponed their final examinations.

stories.title: Elon Musk faces criminal probe in France after ignoring summons in X case
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Elon Musk is under a criminal probe in France related to the X platform.
The French authorities have threatened criminal charges against Musk.
The charges stem from Musk's alleged failure to appear when summoned for questioning.

stories.title: Chrome's 4GB AI model isn't new, but you're not wrong for being confused
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Chrome is utilizing a local 4GB AI model for on-device processing.
The article addresses user confusion regarding the large storage footprint of this local AI feature.
The developer suggests that managing this storage issue is not the end-user's primary responsibility.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
Challenges the conventional MoE design where each transformer layer has a rigid, isolated expert set.
Suggests that decoupling expert allocation from layer depth might be beneficial, based on routing probe analysis.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation of LLMs: their inability to generate novel, valid, and challenging mathematical problems.
Introduces VHG, a new method that uses verification to ensure generated problems are mathematically sound.
Offers an alternative to costly human expert involvement or flawed self-play methods for problem generation in AI training.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The research proposes that using the identical optimizer from the pretraining phase during fine-tuning (SFT) of LLMs is beneficial.
This 'optimizer-model consistency' approach reportedly leads to a better learning-forgetting tradeoff, retaining more knowledge.
The findings suggest this method outperforms other optimizers and even LoRA during the supervised fine-tuning stage.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established benchmark or ground-truth labels exist for a specific context.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to formalize this evaluation setting.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: Current retrieval-augmented agents often treat information retrieval as a 'black box' process.
Existing methods rely on iterative, exploratory querying, mimicking a novice user's search pattern.
The proposed 'Superintelligent Retrieval Agent' aims to incorporate expert-level priors to improve efficiency and reduce latency.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the ambiguity in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG) progress, questioning if reported gains are genuine or due to inconsistent evaluation.
Current MMDG research is noted as being fragmented, with significant variations across datasets, modality configurations, and experimental setups.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for being overly focused on action recognition, neglecting other critical real-world applications.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of optimizing LLM agents for long-horizon decision-making due to purely reactive methods.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework that integrates explicit trajectory-level strategy into agentic RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision-making sequences.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
Challenges the conventional design where each transformer layer has its own isolated expert set.
Suggests that uniform random routing can maintain high accuracy when replacing learned top-k routers in deeper layers.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: LLMs excel at solving problems but struggle to generate novel, valid, and challenging problems for training.
The research introduces VHG, a method designed to generate hard mathematical problems backed by formal verification.
VHG aims to overcome limitations of current methods, which rely on expensive human experts or suffer from reward hacking in self-play.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the practical challenge of comparing LLM safety when no established, labeled benchmarks exist for a specific domain.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to formalize this evaluation setting.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of current retrieval-augmented agents, which treat information retrieval as a 'black box'.
Existing agents use iterative, exploratory querying, mimicking a novice user rather than an expert.
The proposed 'Superintelligent Retrieval Agent' aims to improve efficiency by incorporating strong priors, reducing latency and unnecessary retrieval rounds.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current research suffers from fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across studies.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for focusing too heavily on action recognition while neglecting broader real-world applications.

stories.title: EMO: Pretraining Mixture of Experts for Emergent Modularity
stories.author: Sewon Min
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inefficiency of monolithic Large Language Models (LLMs) by exploring Mixture-of-Experts (MoEs).
A key challenge identified is that restricting inference to a subset of experts for specific domains causes significant performance degradation.
The proposed EMO method aims to improve MoE practicality by enabling emergent modularity within LLMs.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation of LLMs: their inability to generate valid, challenging, and novel mathematical problems.
Introduces VHG, a new method that uses verification to overcome the issues of naive self-play and human dependency in problem generation.
The research aims to advance LLM training and enable more autonomous scientific research capabilities.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established benchmark or ground-truth labels exist.
Proposes a framework for 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' for real-world deployments.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-horizon decision-making in LLM-based agents, which are often purely reactive.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework to incorporate explicit trajectory-level strategies into Agentic RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision-making processes.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Optimization
stories.author: Graham Neubig
bullet_points: Introduces Recursive Agent Optimization (RAO), a new reinforcement learning approach.
RAO is designed to train recursive agents capable of spawning and delegating sub-tasks to themselves.
This recursive structure enables agents to scale to longer contexts and solve complex problems using a divide-and-conquer strategy.

stories.title: Can RL Teach Long-Horizon Reasoning to LLMs? Expressiveness Is Key
stories.author: Abulhair Saparov
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in applying Reinforcement Learning (RL) to LLM reasoning: the lack of controlled, scalable training environments.
Introduces 'ScaleLogic,' a synthetic framework designed to independently control two crucial axes of reasoning difficulty: proof planning depth (horizon) and logical expressiveness.
Focuses on systematically studying how LLM training scales with task difficulty, which is vital for advancing complex reasoning capabilities in LLMs.

stories.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Min Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MASPO, a new framework for optimizing prompts in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS).
Addresses the challenge of aligning local agent objectives with overall system goals in complex collaborative tasks.
Focuses on the critical role of prompt quality in the performance of LLM agents.

stories.title: Google broke reCAPTCHA for de-googled Android users
stories.author: anonymousiam
bullet_points: Google's reCAPTCHA system has reportedly been circumvented or broken for users running de-googled Android devices.
The discussion touches upon the broader context of Google Cloud fraud defense mechanisms.
The story highlights ongoing technical challenges in maintaining security measures against modified or restricted operating environments.

stories.title: Dirty Frag: Universal Linux LPE
stories.author: openwall.com via lattera
bullet_points: The story concerns a vulnerability identified as 'Dirty Frag'.
It is categorized as a Universal Linux Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) exploit.
The source (Lobsters) suggests this is a technical discussion among security researchers.

stories.title: Your AI agent already emits OpenTelemetry. Why aren't you watching it?
stories.author: sunilprakash
bullet_points: The article discusses the built-in OpenTelemetry capabilities of modern AI agent frameworks.
It highlights specific tools like Spring AI, LangChain4j, and Koog as examples of instrumentation.
The core message is a call to action for developers to utilize existing observability features in their AI stacks.

stories.title: I Ran 5 LLMs Through 10 Real Agent Coding Tasks. The Free One Won.
stories.author: vystartasv
bullet_points: The article compares the performance of five different Large Language Models (LLMs) on practical coding tasks.
The evaluation used real-world coding tasks rather than standard competitive programming problems (like LeetCode).
The author concludes that a free LLM outperformed the others in the tested scenarios.
